Please ensure your PR title follows Conventional Commits format (e.g., `feat:`, `fix:`, `docs:`, `chore:`).
Review our [CONTRIBUTING.md](../CONTRIBUTING.md) before submitting.
-->

## 🎯 Description

<!--
Provide a clear and concise summary of the changes.
Explain the **why** behind this PR (Motivation), **what** it achieves, and **how** it is implemented.
-->

### 🔗 Related Issues

<!--
Link any relevant issues or tickets here.
Use keywords to automatically close issues, e.g., `Fixes #123` or `Resolves #456`.
-->

## 🚀 Type of Change

<!-- Please check the option(s) that apply to this PR using "x". -->

- [ ] 🐛 Bugfix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)
- [ ] ✨ Feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality)
- [ ] 💥 Breaking Change (fix or feature that breaks existing functionality)
- [ ] ♻️ Refactoring (changes that neither fix a bug nor add a feature, e.g., code cleanup)
- [ ] 📚 Documentation (changes to docs, README, diagrams)
- [ ] 🔧 Build / CI / Ops (changes to build systems, pipelines, or infrastructure)

## 🧪 Testing Strategy

<!--
Describe how you tested your changes. Provide instructions so reviewers can reproduce the tests.
List any relevant details for your test configuration.
-->

- [ ] Automated tests passed (`make test`)
- [ ] Code hygiene checks passed (`make hygiene`)
- [ ] Manual testing (Please specify what was tested and how):
  -

## ☑️ Pre-Merge Checklist

<!-- Ensure all items are checked off before requesting a review. -->

- [ ] I have performed a self-review of my own code
- [ ] My code follows the repository's coding standards and style guidelines
- [ ] I have commented my code, particularly in hard-to-understand areas
- [ ] I have made corresponding changes to the documentation (including `INDEX.md`)
- [ ] I have added/updated tests that prove my fix is effective or that my feature works
- [ ] I have updated the `CHANGELOG.md` with a summary of the changes
- [ ] If this PR introduces an architectural change, an ADR was added or referenced

## 🔒 Security Audit

- [ ] I have verified that this change does not introduce any security vulnerabilities
- [ ] I have ensured that no secrets, absolute paths, or PII are committed
- [ ] Any new dependencies have been vetted and scanned (`make security-audit` runs locally)

<!-- Thank you for contributing to eco-commander! -->
